definition_short: Real kemp jewellery is Golden Collections' premium South Indian temple and dance jewellery range for Bharatanatyam, Kuchipudi, bridal classical styling, and traditional festive use, made with Kemp or Kempu stones and a richer material/finish direction than regular dance jewellery.
buyer_warning: Do not confuse this with Golden Collections' regular Bharatanatyam/Kuchipudi range.

definition_short: Kemp stones, also called Kempu stones by many buyers, are the red, green, or white stones customers associate with kemp-style and real kemp temple jewellery for classical dance and traditional styling.
buyer_warning: Use customer-friendly stone language and avoid unsupported technical gemstone claims.

definition_short: A Bharatanatyam jewellery set is a coordinated group of classical dance ornaments chosen around the teacher's list, costume direction, dancer size, comfort, and stage use.
buyer_warning: For arangetram, begin with the teacher's required component list before buying.

definition_short: Kuchipudi jewellery is classical dance jewellery suitable for Kuchipudi when the costume direction and teacher's ornament list call for a South Indian temple jewellery look.
buyer_warning: Confirm the required components because Bharatanatyam and Kuchipudi styling can overlap but teacher lists may differ.

definition_short: Deity jewellery is jewellery and alankaram accessories for god and goddess idols, selected by deity, idol size, ornament placement, and measured product fit.
deity_context: All deity groups by compatibility, size, and placement.
buyer_warning: Do not assume every deity accessory is universal; check deity symbols, idol size, posture, and placement.

definition_short: A deity crown is a head ornament for an idol; the right fit depends on idol height, head or face width, crown style, and how the ornament sits on the idol.
deity_context: Varalakshmi, Lakshmi, Amman, Balaji, Krishna, Ganesha, Shiva, Durga, Murugan, Ayyappa, Hanuman by design and size.
buyer_warning: Crowns need stricter measurement than many ornaments; check crown width, height, depth, style, and idol posture.

definition_short: Vaddanam is a waist belt used in classical dance jewellery and deity alankaram; many shoppers also search for oddiyanam, kamarband, or waist belt depending on region and use.
deity_context: General/common by size and placement unless deity-specific design symbols apply.
buyer_warning: For dancers, check waist/costume fit; for idols, check ornament width and placement.

definition_short: Nethi chutti is a forehead ornament used in Bharatanatyam and Kuchipudi jewellery, often searched by shoppers as maang tikka or tikka.
buyer_warning: Check secure wearing with hair setting and earrings/mattal for performance use.

definition_short: Mattal is an ear chain used with earrings in classical dance jewellery, especially for Bharatanatyam, Kuchipudi, and arangetram stage sets.
buyer_warning: Check comfort and secure attachment for long performance days.

definition_short: A long haram is a longer necklace layer used in classical dance jewellery, real kemp sets, bridal classical styling, and deity chest alankaram.
deity_context: General/common by size and placement unless the design has deity-specific symbols.
buyer_warning: For idols, check chest placement and idol height; for dancers, check how it layers with the short necklace.

definition_short: Alankaram means adornment or decoration in the deity jewellery context; Golden Collections should use it for practical idol ornament selection, sizing, and placement guidance.
deity_context: All deity groups, especially Varalakshmi/Lakshmi/Amman in current authority content.
buyer_warning: Avoid unsupported ritual claims; keep public copy practical and product/fit focused.

definition_short: Varalakshmi alankaram is festival-season Lakshmi or Amman decoration using suitable items such as face, crown, hands and legs, harams, vaddanam, flowers, banana trees, and decor where size and context fit.
buyer_warning: Hands/legs and faces are not universal across all idols; check the product and idol context.

definition_short: Hastham and padam are hand and foot accessories used in Varalakshmi, Lakshmi, or Amman doll and idol decoration contexts.
deity_context: Varalakshmi / Lakshmi / Amman doll or idol context.
buyer_warning: Do not position these as universal deity accessories.

definition_short: Namam is a Vishnu-family forehead mark or accessory associated with deities such as Balaji, Venkateswara, Vishnu, and Perumal.
buyer_warning: Do not describe namam as a generic bindi for all deities.

definition_short: Shanku chakra refers to the conch and discus symbols associated with Vishnu-family deity alankaram, especially Vishnu, Balaji, Venkateswara, and Perumal.
buyer_warning: Keep this Vishnu-family specific unless owner/product data confirms a broader use case.

definition_short: Tripund is a Shiva-family forehead mark or accessory, often searched with vibhuti or thiruneer language.
buyer_warning: Do not position tripund/vibhuti as a generic deity forehead ornament.

definition_short: Thomala is a garland-style deity ornament used in deity alankaram; shoppers may also search for vagamalai, mala, or garland depending on region and product style.
deity_context: Varalakshmi / Lakshmi / Amman and general deity use by size/design where appropriate.
buyer_warning: Avoid fresh-flower ritual claims unless owner-confirmed for the specific content.

definition_short: A short necklace is the close neck layer in classical dance jewellery, often searched as addigai, padakkam, short haram, or short chain.
deity_context: Can appear in deity jewellery by size and placement, but this glossary entry should primarily support dance shopping.
buyer_warning: For arangetram, check how the short necklace layers with the long haram and costume neckline.

definition_short: A headset is the group of head ornaments used in classical dance jewellery, often including nethi chutti and side or head pieces depending on the set.
buyer_warning: Confirm the teacher's required pieces because headset contents can vary by set and costume style.

definition_short: Surya chandra refers to sun and moon head ornaments used in classical dance styling when the costume or teacher's requirement includes them.
buyer_warning: Not every dance set requires sun and moon pieces; confirm the performance list.

definition_short: Vanki is an arm ornament used in classical dance and bridal classical styling, also searched as bajuband, armlet, or arm band.
buyer_warning: Check dancer size and comfort for stage movement.

definition_short: Rakodi is a hair ornament used around the bun or braid area in classical dance hair styling, depending on the set and costume direction.
buyer_warning: Check whether the chosen hairstyle needs rakodi, jada pieces, or both.

definition_short: Jada is braid jewellery or a braid accessory used in classical dance hair styling, often planned with rakodi, flowers, or jada kuchulu depending on the look.
buyer_warning: Match the jada accessory to the hairstyle and performance requirement.

definition_short: Salangai are ankle bells used by classical dancers; many shoppers also search for ghungroo or ankle bells when buying dance accessories.
buyer_warning: Choose based on dancer level, comfort, teacher preference, and practice/performance use.

definition_short: Regular dance jewellery is Golden Collections' regular Bharatanatyam and Kuchipudi jewellery range, suitable for classes, school programs, early stage use, and budget-conscious performance needs.
buyer_warning: Do not call Golden Collections' regular range imitation kemp in public copy.

definition_short: Black kemp jewellery is a darker kemp-style range for Bharatanatyam and Kuchipudi costume matching, treated as a separate shopping path from real kemp jewellery.
buyer_warning: Do not merge black kemp decisions into every real kemp buying guide; link it as its own range.

definition_short: Temple jewellery is a South Indian style direction used across classical dance jewellery, real kemp jewellery, bridal classical styling, and deity ornament vocabulary.
deity_context: Use carefully for deity ornaments and alankaram where product context supports it.
buyer_warning: Keep history/origin claims conservative unless owner-confirmed and sourced.
